profile.productArea: software that helps small warehouse operators catch inventory drift before it turns into shrinkage
profile.teamSignals: 15-person startup, engineer number six, working directly with both founders, shipping to real customers every week
profile.rawText:
Backend Engineer at Driftlock

We're Driftlock — a 15-person startup building software that helps small
warehouse operators catch inventory drift before it turns into shrinkage.

We're looking for a backend engineer to own our data ingestion pipeline
end to end. You'd be engineer number six, working directly with both
founders, and shipping to real customers every week.

No big-company process here. If you like owning hard problems and talking
to the people who use what you build, we'd love to meet you.

bullets.text: Rebuilt the event ingestion pipeline in Go, cutting p99 latency from 900ms to 120ms
Led the migration of 14 services from a shared Postgres cluster to per-service databases
Shipped the billing reconciliation service handling 2M invoices per month in TypeScript

education.notes: Graduated with honours; thesis on stream processing
